content: I'm trying to get back into a good morning routine. I've been all over the place since moving to Melbourne.
Moving to a new city can definitely disrupt your routines! What's your ideal morning looking like these days?
Well, I usually wake up around 6:30 now. Back in Yellowstone I'd be up at 5:30 for my ranger shift, but here I'm still adjusting.
That's a pretty early start! Do you have a morning ritual that helps you transition into the day?
Yeah, I make coffee and feed Coco first thing. She's my rabbit - got her when I moved here. Then I'll usually practice piano for 20 minutes.
A rabbit named Coco sounds adorable! How long have you been playing piano? That's a nice way to start the day.
I've played since I was a kid, but I'm rusty. I'm trying to get back into it. After piano, I'll make a big breakfast - usually Thai food since I love cooking it.
Thai breakfast? That's impressive! What's your go-to dish? I'm more of a cereal person myself.
Khao tom - it's rice soup with ginger and pork. I make a big pot on Sundays and reheat it through the week. Saves time.
That's smart meal prep! Do you work from home now or commute to an office in Melbourne?
I'm actually between jobs right now. Used to work as a park ranger at Yellowstone, but I'm figuring out what's next. Maybe something with my mechanical keyboard hobby.
A park ranger to keyboard enthusiast is quite a shift! Have you thought about what kind of work you'd want to do with that interest?
